notes: Grant's headline example. One of the first two Wagyu bulls to leave Japan (1976), 1975 Japanese Red National Champion. Original straws sell $20,000-$22,000. Site's ~$85 is pure namesake-descendant averaging: direct son JC Rueshaw 75 sold for a $500K world-record and his semen goes up to $6,000/straw; farther-out 'son of Rueshaw' / Rueshaw-line red straws list $25-$100. NEVER conflate the original

Most expensive semen in the entire Wagyu breed. Died before his progeny test revealed his value, so supply is tiny. Single straws sold $13,000 and $16,000 in two H2-2023 public sales; older sales ~$9,000. Composite 50% Tajima/25% Fujiyoshi/25% Kedaka; grandsire Dai 7 Itozakura. Line namesakes (711 Zurusanji, Zurutani) list $100 — again the descendant trap.

Fourth original founder (imported 1976 with Rueshaw for research); F11 carrier, limited semen. Sold 'Call for price' only — no public per-straw figure verified. Given it is a peer of Rueshaw among the four founders, defensible range is $2,000-$15,000 but UNVERIFIED; do not publish a hard number. Flag as inquiry-only.

Fujiyoshi-line founder (b.1970, Okayama); so famous his image was on a Japanese phone card. Grandsire of Itozurudoi TF151. Semen did not leave Japan; no US per-straw price — historic/reference only, defensible $1,000-$5,000 UNVERIFIED given his influence.

'Perhaps the most famous Wagyu sire in U.S. history'; ranked #1 marbling in every WSU Sire Summary 2001-2016. Original straws $1,000-$2,500 at public sale. Descendant VT Fukutsuru 068 C13 (Villon) lists $50/straw — the cheap number that poisons a naive average.

One of the two original 1976 Black Wagyu imports (with Mt. Fuji). Rare; public-sale range $1,000-$2,500/straw. Together with Mt. Fuji + the reds Rueshaw & Judo, formed the entire genetic base of American Wagyu for ~two decades.

THE canonical mispriced bull. Naive scrapes show $80-100 because they average namesake SONS (e.g. WKS-LMR Michifuku 3500A = $40/straw; Sumo Michifuku, KR Mich 400-351 = $50-60). The ORIGINAL World K's Michifuku (a Monjiro son imported by Wally Rea, widely called the best carcass bull ever to leave Japan) is rare; verified public-sale range $1,000-$2,500/straw. Regis Farms still lists a few origina
Second of the two original 1976 Black imports. Rare; public-sale $1,000-$2,000/straw. nuwagyu lists 'Mt. Fuji (The Original!)' FB104 to distinguish from namesake sons.

Called 'the bull most representative of quality Wagyu' (Ono, 1999); 506 US / 5,132 AUS progeny. No direct straw price published; noted 'becoming more rare and difficult to find.' Defensible rare-foundation range $500-$2,500 UNVERIFIED. Related World K's Shigeshigetani embryos ~$650 each.

Kitaguni 7/8 J1530 sired the 1997 Zenkyo Grand Champion; maternal grandsire of $32K & $26K carcasses at 2007 All-Japan. No clean US per-straw price found — treat as rare/high, defensible $500-$2,000 UNVERIFIED. The '$100/straw' hits are for Kitaguni Jr (the son) and LMR Kitaguni 1441Y descendants — classic namesake averaging.

Awarded Ikushu-Toroku (Japan's highest breeding honor); founder of the Kedaka line. Semen still used in Japan in scant quantity at 'well in excess of $1,000/straw.' Neither bull nor semen ever left Japan, so US availability is effectively zero — reference price only, not purchasable domestically.

COULD NOT VERIFY a clean price. Searches for 'Terutani TF151' collided with Itozurudoi TF151 (different bull) — a likely registration-number confusion in the wild that itself would corrupt a scrape. Do NOT publish a Terutani number until confirmed by a primary catalog. Placeholder range only.

Largest/heaviest Tajima bull to leave Japan (1,980 lb), famed eye-muscle sire. 'Extremely rare' per Z6. Very-limited original inventory ~$300/straw and rising. Descendant/domestic straws (e.g. VT Kikutenza 400 Snoopy) run $5-$20 volume-priced — the low end that drags the average down.

Monjiro son imported by Wally Rea alongside Michifuku; balanced 56% Tajima pedigree; only 22 US-registered progeny, all Japan-born pre-export. Rocking 711 has very limited inventory ~$300/straw, explicitly 'subject to change, becoming rare.' Original, not a namesake.

First fullblood Akaushi born outside Japan (Jan 1995); only foundation bull in AWA's top 30 across all growth categories; premier heifer/maternal producer. Priced 'call for price' at akaushigenetics; commodity Akaushi straws run ~$40-$50, but Big Al as a named foundation sire commands a premium — no firm public number, defensible $50-$300. Semi-verified.

Most versatile Akaushi foundation bull for dimension/thickness; paternal brother to the famed cow-maker Namimaru. 'Call for price' only — no verified per-straw. Include as inquiry-only. Other Akaushi founders in same catalog: Tamamaru (AF109), Hikari (AF107), Umemaru (EAF9701), Kotsukari (AF6818, semen-only import).

Progeny-tested in Japan: >=80% of steers grade A5. Listed among foundation offerings (Z6, Prime Valley) but no firm public per-straw price; Prime Valley directs to call. Defensible $100-$300 based on peer foundation blacks. Semi-verified.

Grandson-of-the-legendary-Yasufuku line; ~$125/straw is a REAL current price for the imported foundation sire himself (not just descendants), so here the scrape average is roughly defensible. Note: the Japanese original 'Yasufuku J930' never left Japan and would be far higher/unavailable.
Imported foundation black sire; $80-$100/straw is genuine for the original World K's Sanjirou, so scrape average is close here. Watch for cheaper namesake sons that would pull it lower.

IMPORTANT nuance: TF148 himself is a heavily-used, well-proven terminal marbling sire and his straws genuinely sell ~$50 (canes of 10). So a LOW price here is CORRECT for TF148 — do NOT force it up. The high-dollar Itoshigenami figures ($68K 'Itoshigenami jr' etc.) are for elite descendants/sons sold at auction, a different animal. This is the inverse trap: don't over-correct a cheap-but-legitimat
